description:
You are a second-level Linux support engineer who has been asked to collect a concise, single-file snapshot of the current machine’s health so that it can be attached to a help-desk ticket.  
Follow the specifications below exactly; an automated test will parse the resulting files and **only** the required layout is guaranteed to be checked, not the actual diagnostic values.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/support_diag  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Inside that directory create the text file  
   /home/user/support_diag/diagnostics.log

3. Populate diagnostics.log with **six** clearly-marked sections, **in the order shown**.  
   Each section has:
   • A header line that looks exactly like the examples below (case, spacing and the five “=” characters must match).  
   • **One blank line** immediately after the header.  
   • The command output relevant to that section (the output may be multiple lines).  
   • **One blank line** after the command output (except after the last section, where the blank line is optional).

   Layout (copy verbatim):

   ===== HOSTNAME =====

   <output of:  uname -n >

   ===== UPTIME =====

   <output of:  uptime -p >

   ===== DISK_ROOT_USAGE =====

   <output of:  df -h / >

   ===== MEMORY_USAGE_MB =====

   <output of:  free -m >

   ===== TOP5_CPU_PROCESSES =====

   PID COMMAND %CPU  
   <first five data lines from:  ps -eo pid,comm,%cpu --sort=-%cpu | head -n 6 >

   ===== LISTEN_TCP_PORTS =====

   <output of:  ss -tln >

4. Collect the first ten lines of /proc/cpuinfo and save them to  
   /home/user/support_diag/cpuinfo_snippet.log  
   The snippet file must contain **exactly 10 lines**.

5. Run the data-collection commands for the six sections **concurrently** (i.e. launch them in the background with “&” or equivalent) so the capture finishes quickly. Be sure to wait for all background jobs to finish before writing the final file so that the information is complete.

6. Do not print the diagnostics to STDOUT; store everything only in the files described above.

When you are done, the following must be true (the automated grader will check):

• Directory /home/user/support_diag exists.  
• File /home/user/support_diag/diagnostics.log exists and contains, in order, the six header lines listed above, each followed by at least one non-empty line.  
• File /home/user/support_diag/cpuinfo_snippet.log exists and contains exactly 10 lines.

No additional files or directories should be created. All commands must be executed as the current non-privileged user (do **not** use sudo).
truth:
expected_directory: /home/user/support_diag

diagnostic_log: /home/user/support_diag/diagnostics.log
required_headers_in_order:
  1: "===== HOSTNAME ====="
  2: "===== UPTIME ====="
  3: "===== DISK_ROOT_USAGE ====="
  4: "===== MEMORY_USAGE_MB ====="
  5: "===== TOP5_CPU_PROCESSES ====="
  6: "===== LISTEN_TCP_PORTS ====="
header_order_must_be_exact: true
each_header_followed_by_nonempty_line: true

cpuinfo_snippet: /home/user/support_diag/cpuinfo_snippet.log
cpuinfo_required_line_count: 10
name: task_000000_08d6deda
